lines.text: A Theatre, Chandler and Ross are there to watch the premiere of Kathy's play.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, she is the star of the play. And she is my girlfriend! I get to have sex with the star of the play!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: People can hear you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I know!!
The play starts.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wow! She looks great. Doesn't she?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.
Onstage there's a knock on the door and Kathy opens it. We don't see what's going on, only hear it.

lines.speaker: Kathy's Co-Star
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Kathy
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That is one good looking man!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Is it just me, or can you actually see his abs through his overcoat?!

lines.speaker: Kathy's Co-Star
lines.text: Sooo, you've been doing this long?

lines.speaker: Kathy
lines.text: No, you're my first. Put the money on the table.
Ross and Chandler have stunned looks on their faces as Kathy and her co-star start making out.

lines.speaker: Kathy's Co-Star
lines.text: Oh, yeah! Ooh, that's nice. (They start making out harder.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Dude!

lines.speaker: Kathy's Co-Star
lines.text: Is that an expensive blouse?

lines.speaker: Kathy
lines.text: If you want it to be.
Kathy's co-star rips her blouse off and buttons go flying into the audience, and one hits Ross. Chandler's mouth is on the floor.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Here's your girlfriend's button. (Holding the button.)
Opening Credits
Chandler and Joey's, erm, Monica and Rachel's, Monica is still unpacking after the move, and Phoebe is their also.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, hey, Mon, do you still have your like old blouses and dresses from high school?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, I think I have some around here somewhere. Why?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, it's just that maternity clothes are so expensive.
Rachel enters

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, Rach! I made a pile of your stuff over on this side of the room. If you could just...(Rachel grunts and throws her purse at it)...throw your purses at it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Bloomingdale's eliminated my department. (Phoebe gasps)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God, are you out of a job?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, but they stuck me in personal shopping. Which is just a huge step down!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Personal shopping? What is that? Like where you walk around with snooty rich people and tell them what to buy?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh-huh.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That sounds great!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering) Hey! (Goes to the fridge.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Umm, excuse me, we switched apartments. You can't eat are food anymore, that-that gravy train had ended.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (Holding a turkey leg) There's gravy?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: If you have the big apartment you have to deal with people coming over all the time. That fridge has got to be stocked, okay, that's your department now. (She takes back the turkey leg)
Joey climbs up on the counter and starts looking at the top of the cabinets.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What are you doing?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I think I left a donut up here.
Central Perk, Ross and Chandler are entering after the play. Phoebe and Joey are already there.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh! How was Kathy's play?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, Kathy gets half-naked and simulates sex with a real good lookin' guy.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, it's like someone literally wrote down my worst nightmare and then charged me $32 to see it!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That's a good idea for a business!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm totally screwed. Okay, they are gonna be hot and heavy on stage every night, and then they're gonna go to their cast parties and he's gonna try to undermine me. Y'know it'll be like, "So where's your boyfriend, what's-his-name, Chester?" And she'll go, "No-no-no, it's Chandler." And he'll go, "Whatever. Ha-ha-ha-ha!"

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (to Ross) That-that is a good trick.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right, look, look, what am I gonna do?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Chandler, look they're actors. They're there to do a job, just 'cause they work together, doesn't mean they're gonna get together. I mean just 'cause it happened with Susan Sarandon and Tim Robbins, it doesn't mean it's gonna happen with them.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh-oh, Alec Baldwin and Kim Basinger.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Tom Cruise and Nicole Kidman.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey, Mel Gibson and Clint Eastwood.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: They're not a couple!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh-okay, I get the game now.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay look, look, let me ask you a question, when they were doing it on stage, was it like really hot?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh yeah!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well okay, so then you're fine. The rule is when two actors are actually doing it off-stage all the sexual tension between them is gone. Okay? So as long as it's hot onstage you got nothing to worry about. It's when the heat goes away, that's when you're in trouble.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Look, you guys have been to every play I've ever been in, have I ever had chemistry on stage?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Noooo!!
Monica and Rachel's, erm, Chandler and Joey's, the gang minus Monica and Rachel are eating breakfast. Ross and Chandler are sitting at the foosball table and eating.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So uh, man, are you gonna go to the play with me tonight?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Y'know what, I don't know how comfortable I am going to see how hot the sex is between some guy and your girlfriend.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, I know but...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh no-no-no, I'm there.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (entering) Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey Mon! Want some pancakes?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You made pancakes?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yep! Grab a plate.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay. (She does so and starts to walk towards Joey to receive her pancakes.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No-no, stay right there. (Monica stops and Joey flips her a pancake, which flies over her head and lands in the living room.) Gettin' closer.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, okay, but don't worry, because we also have cereals, muffins, waffles, and, jams, jellies, and marmalades. Which I'm fairly certain are the same thing.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Listen also we're uh, we're watching the game here Saturday night, if people want to come over.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh yeah!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, I was thinking about having people over for the game.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh yeah, who's playing?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: The players.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Somebody seems to be missing being the hostess.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (laughs) Please, it's a relief is what it is, is what it is.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right Pheebs, stick out your plate!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh. (Joey flips the pancake and Phoebe catches it and throws it on her plate.)
Bloomingdale's, Rachel's new job.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (on the phone) Monica, I'm quitting! I just helped an 81 year old woman put on a thong and she didn't even buy it! (Pause) I'm telling you I'm quitting! That's it! I'm talking to my boss right now! (Pause) Yes I am! (Pause) Yes I am! Yes I am! Yes I am! Yes I am! Yes I am! Okay bye, call me when you get this message. (Hangs up as her boss, Mr. Waltham, walks in.) Oh! Mr. Waltham, I ah really need to talk to you.

lines.speaker: Mr. Waltham
lines.text: In a moment, please, I'm in the middle of a task. And you have a customer.
It's a really good-looking man, Joshua, that Rachel has an instant crush on. Well actually it's Tate Donovan, so it's not like she's really testing her acting skills.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Hi, I'm Joshua.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi, I'm Rachel Green. What can I do for you Joshua?

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Well, I need a whole new wardrobe. My wife, well my ex-wife...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I'm so sorry.
He walks over to look and some clothes and Rachel quickly turns around and adjusts her bra, trying to show off her assets.

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Anyway, she burned all of my clothes. I got away with two things. This suit and what turned out to be a skirt.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, at least that's a great suit.

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Yeah, but it wasn't much fun dropping it off at the dry cleaners in the skirt. (Rachel laughs) So I need everything down to underwear, so if you're willing, I'm all yours.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Mr. Waltham
lines.text: Rachel, you needed to speak to me?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No-no, that wasn't me! (To Joshua) Well, we should get started. Let me show you my underwear. (Joshua turns at that) The selection of underwear we carry.
He walks out and Rachel stares at his butt as he leaves. He makes a wrong turn.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh-oh, sorry, it's this way, it's this way. (Motions to the correct way.)

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: It's this way? Sorry. (He walks past her and she again admires his butt.)
Kathy's play, Ross and Chandler are waiting for her in the lobby after the play.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm right! Right? There was like no chemistry between them. Before they had heat, and now there's no heat! Now you know what this means, Joey told us what this means!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right, let's not jump to any conclusions. All right? There was some sexual chemistry between them.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Come on, it was like cousins having sex up there!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Here she comes. Don't say a word, okay? Just be cool, don't be...y'know you.

lines.speaker: Kathy
lines.text: Hey you guys!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Kathy
lines.text: Hi! (Kisses Chandler) Thank you so much for coming again. Did you like it tonight?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, absolutely! (Chandler mumbles something.)

lines.speaker: Kathy
lines.text: Wasn't Nick funny when he couldn't get his match lit?
Chandler laughs without opening his mouth.

lines.speaker: Kathy
lines.text: It's a good play, isn't it?
Chandler mumbles something, and Ross tells him to "Come on."

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, I loved the play. You were great, and Nick ditto. Clearly you're having sex with him.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, I... (Walks away.)

lines.speaker: Kathy
lines.text: Clearly, I'm having sex with him?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh come on, it was so obvious! There was no chemistry between you two!

lines.speaker: Kathy
lines.text: Okay, so let me just get this straight. You're accusing me of cheating on you, and insulting my performance?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y'know, I-I could see how this could happen, y'know you're up there every night, you're naked, touching, kissing.

lines.speaker: Kathy
lines.text: Acting! Chandler, this is my job! I'm-I'm playing a part in a play! How can you not trust me?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, you can understand, given how we started.

lines.speaker: Kathy
lines.text: Oh, wow. I can't believe you're throwing that in my face.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, that is what happened, and I don't even see you denying this!

lines.speaker: Kathy
lines.text: I'll tell you what, Chandler, why don't you call me when you grow up!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, well, don't expect that to happen anytime soon!
Commercial Break
Monica and Rachel's, erm, Chandler and Joey's, Joey, Phoebe, and Ross are there eating pizza as Rachel enters.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I have the best job in the entire world! The most adorable guy came over today, and I got to dress him up all day!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Rachel has a new doll.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I wish he was a doll, then I could get a Rachel doll and bump them together and make kissy noises. Oh! And he has the most beautiful name, I never realised it, Joshua! Josh-u-a! Joshua! Josh.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, hello!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi-e!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh, what do I smell?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don't know, it smells good.
He goes over and opens the door to reveal Monica holding a plate of cookies and a fan to blow the smell across the hall.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Fresh cookies! Hot from the oven!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh! (They all go over to her apartment)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Please, have some!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, yumm!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, I've just been fiddling around in here making delicious treats for everyone.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (Holding a magazine) Wow! The new Playboy!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, it's just something I picked up.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Cookies and porn, you're the best mom ever!!
Rachel laughs hysterically for no reason.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What? What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, it was just something Josh said about v-necks, but you had to be there.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, how does Jason look in a v-neck?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It's Joshua.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, whatever. (Laughs and gives Joey the thumbs up heading back to his apartment. Joey follows him)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wait! Wait! This isn't take-out!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (getting up) Well, I hate to eat and run, but...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, wait, please don't go! I've got porn for you too!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, I don't need it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: People are supposed to wanna hang out here!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Why? Honey, what is the big deal?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm the hostess! Not those guys! I'm always the hostess! I mean, I was always the hostess, I mean even when I was little, I mean the girls brought their dollies to my tea party, I-I served the best air.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, why did you make like a whole big thing out of y'know, everyone has to hang out in the big apartment?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: 'Cause they took our apartment, I wanted to punish them. But I'm-I'm done now. They've suffered enough.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: If you wanted to punish them, you should've just made them hang out here!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, that's true.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right then, when I'm done with this place, it's gonna be ten times better than that place!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, are we gonna trash that place?
Chandler comes back, obviously drunk, and trips over the steps.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Steps! (He opens the door to his apartment to Ross and Joey looking at the new Playboy) Slut! (Ross and Joey quickly hide the Playboy behind their backs. Chandler wonders into the girl's apartment.) You will all be very happy to hear that Kathy is sleeping with that guy!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So you were right?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I confronted her, and she didn't deny it! (Pause) I don't live here! (Goes back into his apartment and they all follow him.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Chandler!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Chandler, what did she say?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wait a minute.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Come here.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Are you sure?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I may be drunk, but I know what she said! Then I went over to Beefsteak Julie's...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Beefsteak Charlie's?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes! See you and I have always been like-(motions that they think the same.)
The hallway between the apartments, Monica is lugging one of those floor polishing machines through the hallway. Rachel comes up the steps and stops when she sees Monica.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Whoops. (Starts to go into Chandler and Joey's.) Oh, hey, do you need help with that?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Nah, I got it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ooh, I just feel bad, I never vacuum. (She goes into Chandler and Joey's.)
[Cut to inside the apartment.]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So I was with Joshua for an hour today, and he has not asked me out. It's just so frustrating!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Why don't you ask him out?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, yeah, totally! That's such a turn-on!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Really? It doesn't seem desperate?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh-ooh, that's the turn-on.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: He just got a divorce right?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hmm-mmm.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So he's probably really nervous around women, y'know? Maybe, you just have to make the first move.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah but, I've never asked a guy out before.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (shocked) You've never asked a guy out?!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No. Have you?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Thousands of times!! That doesn't make me sound too good does it?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't even know how I would go about it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh-oh-oh-oh, how I do it is, I look a woman up and down and say, "Hey, how you doin'?"

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, please!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (to Phoebe) Hey, how you doin'?
Phoebe looks at him, and then giggles and looks away.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You know what, I'm gonna do that, I'm gonna call him up, and I'm gonna ask him out. I can do that. Ask him out. (Practising) How you doin'? (Calls him) Hi! Joshua? It's Rachel Green from Bloomingdale's. (Listens) Yeah, umm, I was wondering if you umm, if you umm, left your wallet at the store today? Well, we found a wallet, and we-(Listens) the license? Well, that is a good idea! Uh, well, let's see here this says this license belongs to a uh, uh, belongs to a mister uh, Pheebs, and umm, yeah, so sorry to bother you at home. I'll see you tomorrow. Bye. (Hangs up) (to Phoebe) You've done that a thousand times?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I've never done that.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ohh, God, I just got so nervous that he would say no.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, you gotta give him something that he can't say no too. Like uh, Knicks tickets! Invite the guy to a Knicks game, you're guaranteed he'll say yes!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Really?! You think that will work?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Absolutely! And if it doesn't, can I get the extra ticket?
We hear a noise coming from Monica and Rachel's apartment.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What the heck is that?
They go open the door and reveal Monica being spun around on the floor polisher and getting the cord wrapped around her legs.
Monica and Rachel's, erm, Chandler and Joey's, Ross, Joey, and Phoebe are watching a movie as Chandler comes in from his bedroom.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Did she call?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, sorry.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right, maybe I should call her.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No! Forget her, man! You don't need her, you don't need that!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: He's right, what she did was unforgivable.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, yeah, but y'know, what-what if I was wrong?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: How might you be wrong?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well y'know, what if she didn't actually sleep with the guy?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Dude, tell me she actually told you this.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: She did not have to tell me, I saw the play, and there was no heat. Back me up here, Ross!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's all you're basing this on?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's not backing me up! Look, you said with the off-stage and the heat, and the onstage and the oy heat.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Whoa-whoa, that-that was just a theory! There's a lot of theories that didn't pan out. The lone gunman. Communism. Geometry.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh my God!!
Bloomingdale's, Rachel's new job, she is waiting for Joshua and practising how to ask him out.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Would you like to go to a basketball game with me? (Tries again.) You know, its funny, basketball, because I happen to have tickets too... (Tries again.) Umm, who likes the Knicks-(Joshua comes in from the dressing room.)

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: What do you think?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! Well, as a single woman, who is available, I think you look great!

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Huh. (She starts brushing the lint off and checking him out in the mirror.) Yeah?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yep. Oh, yeah, look you great. (She puts her arm in his and checks how they would look as a couple.) Oh yeah. Yeah, this looks great. (Pause) Umm, so you like it?

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: I do. I do. I love it. In fact, I think I'm gonna wear it home.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Great.

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: All right, thank you so much for all your help.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Sure.

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Well, I guess this is uh, I guess this is it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah-eah-ha!

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Thanks. (He starts to leave) Maybe I'll see in the spring, with the uh, y'know, for the uh, bathing suits.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh well, you don't want to do that now?!

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Ah, that's okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Anyway, hopefully, I'll see you around sometime. (He goes out the door.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Basketball!

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: (coming back in) I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I uh, I have two tickets to the Knicks game tonight if you're interested, just as a thank you for this week.

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Wow! That would be great.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Yeah, that would be fantastic! My-my nephew is crazy about the Knicks! This is fantastic, thank you so much Rachel. (He takes the tickets and leaves as Mr. Waltham returns.)

lines.speaker: Mr. Waltham
lines.text: Good morning.

lines.speaker: Joshua
lines.text: Hi!
Mr. Waltham admires Joshua's butt as he leaves.
Kathy's apartment, Chandler is knocking on the door.

lines.speaker: Kathy
lines.text: (opening the door) Hey.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey. I just, I just wanted to come over to-to say that I'm sorry. Y'know? I know I acted like the biggest idiot in the world, and I can completely understand why you were so upset.

lines.speaker: Kathy
lines.text: Oh wow. I really wish you'd call me.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, I know, I-I wish I had too, but y'know I-I think this is a good thing. Y'know? 'Cause we've had our first fight, and now we can move on. Y'know, I know for me-(Notices a pair of men's pants on the chair.) Nick's pants?

lines.speaker: Kathy
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah. Well, I think our second fight is going to be a big one!
Monica and Rachel's, erm, Chandler and Joey's, Joey is debriefing Rachel on her rejection earlier that day and telling her what she should've done. Ross and Phoebe are watching.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, for next time, what do you say?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I have an extra ticket. An extra ticket. Not, two tickets, I have an extra ticket.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So the first time you ask a guy out, he-he turns you down?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: He didn't turn me down! He's at the game isn't he? I got the date, I'm just not on it!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (entering) Okay, it's ready. Come on.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What's ready?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Just come.
They all go over to her apartment.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wow! Monica!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Great!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: This is beautiful!
She has cleaned it, completely redecorated it, removed the carpet, and polished the floor.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh did you-what did you-did you work for two days straight?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Pretty much. (to Joey) So, what do you, what do you think of the floor?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don't know, it looks the same.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You used to have carpet.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh yeah!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So I made snacks. Please, just hang out okay? I'm just gonna rest my eyes just a little bit.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Look, Mon, do you want us to uh, come back later?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh no-no-no, stay, stay, stay, just keep talking. I'm always the hostess.
Chandler returns.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: How'd it go?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, she wasn't sleeping with him.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh good!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: She is now.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What are you saying?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm saying that she... is a devil woman! Y'know I mean you think you know someone and then they turn around and they sleep with Nick! Nick, with his rock hard pecs, and his giant man-nipples! I hate him, I hate her! Well, I don't hate her, I love her. This is all my fault really.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: How? How is your fault?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Because, I-I should've called! Y'know if I had just called her after our big, stupid fight, she never would've gone out with Nick, and they would've ended up in bed together. I threw her at his man nipples!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Honey, this is not your fault, just because you guys had a fight, it does not justify her sleeping with someone.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, if-if she thought they were on a break...
They all turn and glare at him.
Closing Credits
Bloomingdale's, Rachel is sitting at her desk as Mr. Waltham comes in.

lines.speaker: Mr. Waltham
lines.text: Rachel, one of your customers seems to have left his billfold. A Joshua Bergen.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Mr. Waltham
lines.text: Will you call him?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes! I will! Absolutely! (She takes out his license and her license and holds them face to face with each other. She then proceeds to act like Dark Helmut in Spaceballs, and mimic a conversation between the two of them.) Hello, Rachel. Hi, Joshua. I left my wallet here on purpose. Really? Yes, I just wanted to see you again. Oh, I'm glad. Rachel, I'd like to say something to you. Yes? How you doin'?
End
